zh.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers是Richard de Wevers和Mrs Richard de Wevers的女兒，於1302年出生在英國英格蘭爾瓦布爾頓。她於大約1327年與Geoffrey de Warburton III結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年享年97歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers是Richard de Wevers和Mrs Richard de Wevers的女儿，于1302年出生在英国英格兰爾瓦布爾頓。她于大约1327年与Geoffrey de Warburton III结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年享年97岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wevers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Lever 1 and 2, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Lever
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a fleet-footed or timid person, from Old French levre ‘hare’ (from Latin lepus, genitive leporis). It may also have been a metonymic occupational name for a hunter of hares.

English: topographic name for someone who lived in a place thickly grown with rushes, from Old English lǣfer ‘rush, reed, iris’. Compare Laver . Great and Little Lever in Greater Manchester are named with this word (in a collective sense), so the surname may also be a habitational name from these places.
Dutch and North German: from the medieval personal names Lever, a variant of Liever (see Lieber ), and Levert, a variant of Lievert (see Liebhart ). This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Levis
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Levis (Old English Lēofhyse, from lēof ‘dear, beloved’ + hyse ‘youth, warrior’).

English: occasionally perhaps from the Middle English personal name Leve, Old English Lēofa (male) or Lēofe (female), which derive from lēof ‘dear, beloved’, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, since Leve is attested as a medieval surname.
English: perhaps also from Middle English lef, leve, plural leves, possibly representing Old English lēaf ‘leaf, foliage’ in some topographic sense, or Old English hlīf ‘shelter’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers was born in 1302, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Richard de Wevers and Mrs Richard de Wevers. She married Geoffrey de Warburton III about 1327. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1399, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers est née en 1302, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Richard de Wevers et Mrs Richard de Wevers. Elle a épousé Geoffrey de Warburton III environ 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1399, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers wurde 1302 in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von Richard de Wevers und Mrs Richard de Wevers geboren. Sie hat um 1327 Geoffrey de Warburton III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1399 im Alter von 97 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers è nata il nel 1302 nel Warburton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di Richard de Wevers e Mrs Richard de Wevers. Lei sposato Geoffrey de Warburton III circa 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1399, all'età di 97 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice De Weversは1302年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアワーブルトンでRichard de WeversとMrs Richard de Weversの娘として生まれた。約1327年にGeoffrey de Warburton IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年に97歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers(은)는 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 워버턴에서 Richard de Wevers(와)과 Mrs Richard de Wevers의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1327년 Geoffrey de Warburton III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 97세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers nasceu em 1302, em Warburton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de Richard de Wevers e Mrs Richard de Wevers. Ela casou-se com Geoffrey de Warburton III aproximadamente 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1399, com 97 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers родилась в 1302 г. в местности варбуртон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Richard de Wevers и Mrs Richard de Wevers. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey de Warburton III около 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1399 г. в возрасте 97 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Alice De Wevers nació en 1302, en Warburton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de Richard de Wevers y Mrs Richard de Wevers. Se casó con Geoffrey de Warburton III aproximadamente 1327. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1399, a la edad de 97 años.
